heading: The excepted service
label: 2103 The excepted service
notes: Historical and Revision NotesThe section is supplied for convenience. The “excepted service” has come to mean all employees not in the competitive service, for whatever reason.
Amendments1978—Subsec. (a). inserted reference to Senior Executive Service.Pub. L. 95–454

Effective Date of 1978 AmendmentAmendment by effective 9 months after , and congressional review of provisions of sections 401 through 412 of , see , set out as an Effective Date note under .Pub. L. 95–454Pub. L. 95–454section 415 of Pub. L. 95–454section 3131 of this titleOct. 13, 1978
amendments: Amendments1978—Subsec. (a). inserted reference to Senior Executive Service.Pub. L. 95–454

Effective Date of 1978 AmendmentAmendment by effective 9 months after , and congressional review of provisions of sections 401 through 412 of , see , set out as an Effective Date note under .Pub. L. 95–454Pub. L. 95–454section 415 of Pub. L. 95–454section 3131 of this titleOct. 13, 1978

children.label: a
children.text: For the purpose of this title, the “excepted service” consists of those civil service positions which are not in the competitive service or the Senior Executive Service.

children.label: b
children.text: As used in other Acts of Congress, “unclassified civil service” or “unclassified service” means the “excepted service”.
